title: Energy stress and real activity
description: Energy affordability is shown beside production, manufacturing, spending, investment, and GDP growth to inspect transmission rather than assume it.
plainLanguageSummary: Energy affordability is shown beside production, manufacturing, spending, investment, and GDP growth to inspect transmission rather than assume it.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Growth rates are year-over-year; energy burden is the expenditure share of nominal GDP.
calculation.explanation: Common recessions, monetary policy, credit, productivity, and supply shocks can drive several series together.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Common recessions, monetary policy, credit, productivity, and supply shocks can drive several series together.

methodology.formula: Growth rates are year-over-year; energy burden is the expenditure share of nominal GDP.
methodology.notes: Common recessions, monetary policy, credit, productivity, and supply shocks can drive several series together.
